channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: Why aren't you holding BTC? — 60 replies — 105.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
Why aren't you holding BTC?

Open thread • Replies: 60 • Updated: 2026-06-25 19:52 UTC

OP

Too expensive? Prefer alts? Don't trust it? Or do you simply think there are better opportunities elsewhere?
Curious to hear your thoughts.

Early replies (first 3)

62395695
you can get better gains on stocks. those companies work for your money. btc sits there doing nothing but has the same risk profile as sharing a hobos needle without greater fools left to carry it to $200k

62395915
give me reasons to hold btc.

62395925
I am holding because I bought in 2020 before etfs now I refuse to sell because I'll get audit raped by the CRA supposedly so I'm stuck holding until it's got better tax laws or borrowing rates.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62404122
If you're not holding BTC when it is clear that the fiat system is on its last days you are beyond retarded.

62404297
>>62395655

Big Thai Cock?
Well I mean like why aren't YOU holding it?
I mean.

62404304
>why aren't you holding goy gold
/biz/ is already out of crypto.
https://web.archive.org/web/202602162103...f-bitcoin/

62404487
>>62404304
Lol

62404614
>>62404122
Why do you need fiat?

62405784
>>62401190
Why though? If you can earn from your BTC while maintaining self-custody, I don't see the problem.

62405818
>>62398819
If it's still BTC and I still control the keys, I don't see why it stops being Bitcoin.

62406014
>>62395655
>Why aren't you holding BTC?
I will buy when it's cheaper. 100 bucks seems like a fair value for the future of finance.

62406579
>>62399676
I guess not but..

62406783
There is simply no reason for it to have any value boss.

62409051
>>62399898
You are a fucking retard. The current price means NOTHING. Its NOTHING! Lower the better to buy more.
Boomers are still in the seats of power. They dont embrace BTC because most boomers not in some kind of power see it as not something they can walk into a bank and ask their financial advisor to get. Therefore its fake and gay to them.
The boomers being the biggest voting block, are the most catered to. So basically the reason why the world has not been embracing digital is because of these fucking idiots.
Once boomers are out of the seats of power and fucking gone and millennials are in charge its going to get real digital.BTC will inevitably be embraced.
More countries will just adopt it as their main currency like El Salvador. Its the best logical option. Your economy is crumbling and the nations dollar is shit? Just adopt bitcoin its quite simple.

Not only that, but owning a Bitcoin will be so rare, everything will cost in Satoshis. Once Amazon adopts a bitcoin logo payment option its 10000% on. Once Musk post some stupid twitter picture of a moon bitcoin mining farm its going to moon.

Get it now while its cheap. Price has no meaning at the moment. If your a day trader I get why you may be pessimistic about it. But if your a hodler and believe in BTC Philosophically and not thinking of it like a share/stock then thats the right idea.

BTC is known by everyone. If you went to what ever hood you are in and asked the most wet cigarette smelling schizo hobo " Hey have you heard of that digital gold thing" the response would be some schizo babble with "That bitcoin stuff:"

Its backed by million computers. It takes IRL power/electrical energy and time (mining) to produce. Thats what money realy is or better to say what gives something value. Time+Energy. Its basically electricity converted into money. And electricity is not free.

HODL is the right idea for a boomerless digital embraced future. dont let Day Traders do it for quick buck not future.

62409106
>>62395695
>"those companies work for your money."
>babby's first dable on stocks
>doesn't know owners are diluting your investment and buying mansions with the ATM

62409107
>>62399898
To add to this, You are thinking of like 10x+ full BTC holders. Average persons goal is to have a single one or 2 to just hold on to the future.
The scenario you are talking about is like for some kind of rich guy living on the lamb which is like 1 out of 10 million people.

The real persons goal is to get a bitcoin and maybe it will be like 300,000 per coin in the future. Then you have enough for your familys college or whatever. Just a good foundation for a son/daughters future. Not somthing you just cash out to cartoonishly and buy lambos with and post videos of your garage with hexagon light ceilings.

62409236
>>62405784
You know it's a risky move.

62409560
>>62409236
Depends what you mean by risky. I wouldn't touch an unaudited protocol, but I also don't think every new protocol carries the same level of risk.

62409564
>>62399898
Governments taxing something doesn't make it dead. If anything, it usually means they've accepted it's an asset class that's here to stay.

62409803
>>62409560
More like defi hacks

62409813
>>62409107
It's possible 10 years from now. Pass it to your kids

62409997
>>62409803
That's why I'm watching Babylon instead of blindly aping into random protocols. They've been taking audits and testing seriously.

62410003
>>62406014
People have gone broke waiting for the perfect entry just as often as they've bought too early.

62410039
>>62410003
So what's the best time to buy

62410525
>>62410039
everytime, imo.

62411160
>>62410525
I don't have money

62411213
>>62409997
I think their TBVs could unlock a lot of Bitcoin use cases that weren't really possible before. We'll know a lot more once the Aave V4 public testnet is complete tho.

62411224
>>62409051
Pls summary

62411241
>>62396002
What About gold and silver?

62411256
>>62404304
I'm not convinced .

62411265
I will just keep my 0.3 btc somewhere

62411638
>>62395915
Institutions are getting onboard, It might do 10X in a decade, also lending is starting to become more relevant with the likes of TBV, there are so many utilities untapped.

62412797
>>62411160
go to work

item.title: UNDER $10K NET WORTH THREAD #1 — 158 replies — 78.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
UNDER $10K NET WORTH THREAD #1

Open thread • Replies: 158 • Updated: 2026-06-25 18:25 UTC

OP

Brothers,

SpaceX has begun to rise. Its success or fall is of little importance to us in the short term.

In the meanwhile we must devise ways to gain a spot at the table with our kin in the six figure club.

I for one propose we spend our time learning a subject. Knowledge is the greatest wealth of all.

However, I would be remiss to not tell the truth that I haven't a clue what we should study.

So instead I suggest we create an eggregore for these threads. Others made monero-chan, bobo and sminem

I am thinking we create one based on a girl who would be willing to believe in us while we are poor and brave the pain throughout having to witness our more disappointing moments of action. Never losing faith in us, she is always there. Yes. This is how we shall spend our time.

At any rate, that is my suggestion for tonight's fun. I have to work in the morning so let's not get too rowdy. Take it or leave it. If you will take it, then be sure to double it and pass it on to the next anon.

Let us commence the general!

Come one, come all!

Early replies (first 3)

62373772
>>62373752
Jannies will surely delete these threads. Instead of creating an eggregore why not try something new and usurp an old one. It may be a derivative of one which has already been made.

62373798
>>62373772
Hello anon thanks for joining. You are poster #1. Your anime image shall be the eggregore. The "coffee" in the image is rather a good omen. We all know images with hot girls accompanied by a brief post on coffee attracts all the attention.

Now that matter is settled, help me add some links to this post here. This will be the header for the start of each general

Helpful links

>Educational materials
https://w9.kaijimanga.com/

>Looking to try the TCG route?
https://youtube.com/@alphainvestments69?si=tDAPjnRSZhyCIGP5

>Lost all hope?
https://www.indeed.com/


>Hidden in plain sight
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Limitless_(film)

>Games to strengthen you financial acumen
https://mahjongsoul.yo-star.com/

>Learn your from your betters
https://ia600609.us.archive.org/3/items/the-house-of-rothschild-ferguson-niall/The%20House%20of%20Rothschild%20-%20Ferguson%2C%20Niall.pdf
What is the name of that character in your post?

62373799
>>62373772
verily, a true fact

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62398868
>>62398799
You are better off getting a job

62398876
>>62398868
https://voca.ro/1aI6fGYlNRNN

62398905
>>62394942
Don’t get that either. Even if you only bought full shares how could you not afford 200 dollars worth of shares? Is this under 10k net worth or under 100$ net worth

62399272
>>62398789
>Wavelength (λ) (THE SYMBOL IN HALF LIFE)

Distance between repeating points of a wave (e.g., crest to crest). Determines frequency and, for light, color.

>Amplitude:

Maximum height of the wave. Determines intensity (brightness for light, loudness for sound).

>Phase

Position of a wave within its cycle. Determines how waves add or cancel when combined.

>Polarization

Direction of oscillation of a transverse wave. For light, it is the orientation of the electric field.

62399358
Tomorrow we shall give predictive programming a try as photonics will likely be a difficult venture

62399394
10k- networth reporting in! You guys are too smart for me with all those grand ideas.

62400367
>>62398789
tl;dr

62401110
7 figure club. I already lost more than 10k today at market open. Stay poor fags

62401765
I just wrote a super long post with all sorts of trade basics and an emphasis for this group to define normal position exit conditions so you can tangibly ask anons "did you exit before, during, or after the standardized 4chan-exit signals?" Along with recommending covered calls for slow moving but trend holding markets like gold/BTc/xmr or oil when watching forex markets. 10y+dxy are good to watch too.
Blah blah blah
But this whole thread degenerated into trying to sell "art", and not every anon working together for every group success to contribute to all that participate, but rather instead for every anon to work on their own project, by themselves, but as a group selling the same products/watering down their labor's value.

>>62401110
This is who you should ask for advice. This anon only lost 1% of his shit n that's if he's only got 1m. Not a big deal. Don't you anons want to be able to lose 10k casually n be unbothered yet responsible?

62401809
>>62401765
I did it the hard way. Degree in finance. Work in finance. Almost 20 years. Been investing since the 08 crash. There's no quick trick or easy money. Crypto is bullshit. Just keep buying.

62401893
Six figures is truly hell, I miss being proud of 70k.

62403333
>>62401765
It was one art guy

62403337
>>62401765
This general also is a work in progress, I am hoping 10 generals from now we have a much better work flow, plan and ultimately success. In some capacity.

62404260
Bump

62404574
No point in this general. Fastest way to make 10k is just to work for a bit, you shouldn’t be investing

62404709
Bump

62404737
>>62373752
its a spacex thread?
its time to buy o better buy in few days one week

62405715
bump

62407354
Page 8 bump, /biz/ must be losing money again because there's an uptick in activity.

62407450
almost 9 months since i started my first job after college
this feels stagnant and joyless
what exactly do i need to do to escape wageslavery and poverty

62407931
Someone send me ten grand so I can join the club, thanks

62408558
>>62407450
What were you doing in 2017, 2021 and December of 2024?

62408565
>>62407354
Thank you, my wage job prevents me from dedicating my energies to this thread but I should have some time soon

62408611
>>62408584
Wtf are you on about dude, yes I made 54 posts over the span of 8 days. I'm surprised you're not taking your own advice and leaving 4chan.

62410044
Bump

62410051
Under 10k you just need to have a job and save cash.
You need cash flow and if you dont have shit a job is the only way to start.

62410731
Bump

62411520
>>62408558
>2017
still in high school
>2021
still in uni
>December of 2024
just graduated and was looking for jobs (i still had 0 dollars to my name at that point)

62411614
>>62373752
>>62373772
>>62373798
>>62373799
>>62373803
>post Maccari's "Cicero Denounces Catiline" (1888)
>crop out Catiline
Fuck you OP, I'm glad you're poor.

62412314
>>62411520
You could've played the memecoin casino in 2021.

item.title: Why do side hustles suck so fucking much? — 22 replies — 72.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Why do side hustles suck so fucking much?

Open thread • Replies: 22 • Updated: 2026-06-25 20:08 UTC

OP

ALL of them are either
>Guaranteed to work below minimum wage
Or
>1% chance to have a good source of additional income but only after you put thousands of hours and years into it
Like why not just work overtime or request additional hours? Seems infinitely more reliable and more worth your time

Early replies (first 3)

62409369
>>62409347
l3l I was just thinking about this speedrun yesterday for some unknown reason. Stay out of my fucking head you nasty pervert.

62409375
>>62409347
Guy on the left is still autistic and the guy on the right is a woman now

62409383
>>62409347
The whole concept of a side hustle is a jewish scam to convince retards to work for pennies

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62409391
>>62409347
Side hustles are dumb. Just work at an hospital in the kitchen or in maintenance. Pay's pretty decent and the schedule is mad flexible

62409399
i think side hustles are for people whose jobs are strictly 9-5 mon-friday and they have no ability to take up more shifts


like for example, i know some health care workers who take as many shifts as physically possible, so they can make as much money as possible, where as some of them will only take a couple shifts per week

You will make more money doing ur same job for more hours than u ever will with a side hustle in 99% of cases, but ur job has to allow u to work more.

62409421
>>62409347
This thread looks fun

62409426
>>62409347
>side hustles
You're basically scraping the dingleberries off capitalism's hairy asscrack

62409443
You're better off maxxing your primary career and investing more

62409517
the main reason to not work more hours is the more you work someplace the worse you get treated. familiarity breeds contempt. so you work somewhere else so your boss doesn't see you so much and find something wrong with you that they will nag you about every day for the rest of your life

62409626
I love my side hustle

I get flown out to the Olympic games or soccer world cups every few years to do some technical work for okay money (15k$ / month) and then go back to my boring ass fulltime job. Just came back from Houston, Texas. World Cup.

62409628
Because it's good social media clickbait. The only ones making serious money mouthing off about side hustles are the content creators like Ramsey Solutions, etc.

62409743
Some side hustles are great, Anon. I had a side business fixing small engines in college off of a Facebook marketplace post. Made GREAT money and as a side benefit I always had a toy in the garage I got on a deal I could flex in the bros with.

Stop bitching on the internet and try harder.

62409748
>>62409626
I really doubt professional footballers would pay some /biz/ poster to suck their dick a few times for $15k only for you to go back to the trailer park you live in outside of Houston blowing guys for 50 bucks a pop.

62409755
>>62409383
I agree that's why they gave it a "cool name"

62409774
the idea that you can make more money while not working full time trying to weasle into someone else's turf who works full time is retarded, no wonder it's so popular among normies

62409878
>>62409748
desu it kinda makes me proud that what is literally my normals life sounds so outlandish to you that you can't even fathom someone doing these things

Literally have made it

62409946
>>62409347
>only after you put thousands of hours and years into it
Do you think it’s any different for literally everything in life? lol

By the way, working 8 hours per day every day for one year on your side hustle = almost 3,000 hours of work already

>Like why not just work overtime or request additional hours?
Ooooh please master give me extra little crumbs of your big cake, I’ve been such a good boy lately, I don’t deserve any freedom or respect but I might deserve these crumbs

62409951
im NEET so everythig i do is a sidehustle. its ok becuase wagecucks subsidize me through gibbs like free internet, discounted utilities, $850 in foodstamps every month, and 100% free health insurance. their quality of life sucks, mine is the same but im not running around with 220 hg bloodpressure every morning and afternoon clawing my way through masses of brown invaders and fellow wagecucks to do performative menial labor for mr shekelburg while making sure to fake socialize with office politics to not to give the HR roastie the ick and get me fired

62412436
>>62409383
>>62409399
>>62409426
>>62409443
this

>>62409743
>I had
>in college
It can be good temporarily if you're in the right place, right time, but long term it's shit and the time would be better spent building a real career.

62412455
>>62409347
>Why do side hustles suck so fucking much?
literally because if they didn't suck they'd be main careers.

>what's your side hustle?
>I'm a freelance pediatric neurosurgeon on weekends

If a side hustle pays good people are just going to do it full time and it's not a side hustle anymore. By definition side hustles have to suck.

62412543
>>62409347
My side hustle of scraping off fomo stocks every week or so have been going well. So has my pokemon card empire.

Must be a skill issue.

62412899
>>62409626
Whats your side hustle? That's my dream job

item.title: In the last month, BTC is down 25% HYPE is down 0.5%. — 14 replies — 56.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
In the last month, BTC is down 25% HYPE is down 0.5%.

Open thread • Replies: 14 • Updated: 2026-06-25 20:15 UTC

OP

In the last month, BTC is down 25%
HYPE is down 0.5%.

Yeah I think I know what I'll be holding from now till the end of the next bullrun. Get a clue, will you?

Early replies (first 3)

62412249
>>62412162
Something to think about

62412308
hype can flip eth
the seethe this would cause is immense
it would actually help ethereum overall, the retarded voices who egotrip all the time would be silenced
bankless dudes would quit
trent would delete his account
anthroposissy would jump off a bridge

62412369
>>62412249
Yeah, im thinkin that Hype is barely down while Daddy BTC is shitting the bed. Are you daft or just a nigger?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62412583
>>62412369
Cope chigger hype back to 3$

62412648
I told you many moons ago but none of you listened. HYPE is a coin that it's gonna be really hard to make it dump hard.

Fees are used for buybacks. And everytime there is a happening, the hyperliquid blockchain is more used, happenings sometimes mean people trading commodities, stocks, whatever. Now there's an outcome markets, people are betting on the stupid worldcup among other things.

It's one of the few blockchains that is actually used. Who knows what will happen, it could get hacked, who the fuck knows, but right now, if things keep going like this, it will surpass ethereum.

62412734
>>62412648
It's crazy how shit biz has become. These brownoids have no foresight.

62412740
>>62412162
20% circulated.

62412786
>>62412740
it's very obvious you dont know anything about the tokenomics. I wont bother explaining here cuz you're all poor low IQ brownies anyway

62412822
>>62412786
Ok saar hyderabad very good coin, aped lifesavings blody!

62412829
CZ bought the presidential pardon and still has $100B to buy other favors. Jeff will meet the same fate as SBF.

62412847
>>62412162
>Get a clue, will you?
Why are there so many super obvious indians on /biz/ all of a sudden?

62412895
>>62412847
Indians dont post on /biz/ anymore tardie, they've been on twitter for 3 years now

62412914
Hype good coin saaarrrr
Fuck off faggot shills!

62412929
>>62412914
Lol indian coins are holding the line while BTC shit coin is dumping like PISS. DRINK UP THE PISS BLOODY BENCHOD

item.title: LET'S FUCKING GOOOOOOOOO — 11 replies — 44.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
LET'S FUCKING GOOOOOOOOO

Open thread • Replies: 11 • Updated: 2026-06-25 20:15 UTC

OP

LET'S FUCKING GOOOOOOOOO

62411659

many riches coming to your way soon saar

Greed ID you'll wagmi it

62412778
>>62411646
Too early, 55k eom.

62412802
40K October 2026

62412858
>>62411646

62412867
>>62412858
you lost so much fucking money you stupid cuckold

62412871
>>62412867
oh shit i struck a nerve you're seething

62412911

Satoshi Kings it is our time.

62412927
>>62412867
Link really does live rent free in your head

item.title: COCA COLA X XRP??? — 7 replies — 28.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
COCA COLA X XRP???

Open thread • Replies: 7 • Updated: 2026-06-25 20:14 UTC

OP

>its real

Early replies (first 3)

62412762
You are Not. Bullish. Enough.

62412770
>>62412754
How old is this carrot on a stick? 12? Lol

62412775
KABOOOOOOOOOM
SO BULLISH XRP

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62412813
>>62412754
yes, what with Cokes largest shareholder being such a famed crypto enthusiast and all.

62412846

Is he not a money enthusiast?
Holy disingenuous fud you fucking clanker

62412855

HOLY BOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM!! SO BULLISH ON XRP BROS!!

BOOOOOOOOOOOOMMMM

item.title: I still can’t believe you stupid assholes spammed Nazi frog memes to shit up official chainlink stuff to where they had… — 5 replies — 20.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
I still can’t believe you stupid assholes spammed Nazi frog memes to shit up official chainlink stuff to where they had…

Open thread • Replies: 5 • Updated: 2026-06-25 20:15 UTC

OP

I still can’t believe you stupid assholes spammed Nazi frog memes to shit up official chainlink stuff to where they had to specifically change shit up to make it out to be that they aren’t associated with 4chan.

It fucking Hitler himself bought into the ICO he wouldn’t have done anything nearly as gay as that. And it’s probably to the detriment of our investment. I hope they chain you faggots up at the yacht party and throw you overboard like in the movie Amistad.

Early replies (first 3)

62412604
Have they used that cube in any official marketing since May 2018? It’s incredible how that image pervades despite the rebrand over 8 years ago.

62412715
>>62412582
What are you, unironically, that you ask such questions? banker? 33rd degree mason? are we getting close?

62412742
LINK would never have survived its first bear market if it wasn't for the fanatics on /biz/. It would have gone the exact same way REQ did

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62412925
>>62412715
>that you ask such questions?
There is not a single question in the post

62412931
>>62412582
The Nazi frog spam will continue until the price improves

item.title: CSAM kills Bitcoin — 16 replies — 19.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
CSAM kills Bitcoin

Open thread • Replies: 16 • Updated: 2026-06-25 19:37 UTC

OP

The first transaction that includes CSAM and is replicated across nodes will permanently tank Bitcoin's price.

The clock is ticking.

Early replies (first 3)

62410005
>>62410002
Adam looks glow, Gaylor is glowing too, is Lee a glow operation too?
Btvlw wtf is this in non alien vitalik language?

62410008
the missing use case already kills BTC lol. that is just desecrating the corpse

62410011
>>62410002
Not to mention once its committed it will be there forever, making the blockchain illegal to possess.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62410036
>>62410002
it's fucking over

62410149
>>62410002
>The first transaction that includes CSAM
You assume this hasn't happened before?

62410186
>>62410002
The guy who was paid by Epstein and went to his island to probably rape children warns that OP_RETURN, a central key of the global chain, may attract child porn. lol

BSV is Bitcoin

62410228
>>62410149
>80 bytes
attached is a 1x1 pixel white square at maximum compression. it is 1228 bytes. so no.

62410231
>>62410002
you are weird

62410239
>>62410228
I could jerk off to that. I grew up in the 90s.

62410241
>>62410228
It's 628B you nigger

62410243
>>62410228
Sure, for a 32 bit color image with headers.

80 bytes could hold a raw 8 bit black and white image with 80 pixels. With compression possibly slightly larger. One bit mono image could be be greater than 640 pixels with compression. Over multiple transactions you could definitely upload a picture that can be pieced back together pretty easily.

62410689
>>62410002
Why didn't it tank fiat then?

62410991
>>62410002
Imagine thinking that something that relies on "developers" is going to have long term value.

62412661
>>62410241
weird, 4chins compressed it better than gimp

>>62410243
>mono image spread over 64 separate transactions
>pretty easily
that's not "easily" contrarian autism guy
easily is 1 picture 1 transaction, viewable in a metadata viewer

62412701
>>62410243
>>62412661
Actually I didn't even math it right. 640x480 image 80 pixels at a time would be 3840 transactions. and you'd need a list of the exact sequence of those 3840 transactions and a custom script to rip and compose the metadata. and picrel would be the result.

62412723
>>62412701
Versus the new format rolled out Oct 2025 which allows for 100kb of metadata per transaction, and would allow a SINGLE transaction to have a photo this size and quality

item.title: Who uses Polkadot? — 4 replies — 16.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Who uses Polkadot?

Open thread • Replies: 4 • Updated: 2026-06-25 20:20 UTC

OP

With the Cardano dApp ecosystem completely imploding from the TapTools closure and the SecondFi exploit, it’s completely obvious Substrate is the only architectural survivor.

Cardano-cels thought Haskell was safe, but asynchronous backing and Polkadot 2.0 Agile Coretime prove that modular shared security is the only way to scale without draining user wallets. Now that the DOT treasury is finally pivoting away from multi-million dollar logo rebranding and actually funding core developer infrastructure, the ecosystem metrics are completely undeniable.

Why aren't you loading up on DOT at these prices before the parachain coretime marketplace goes fully liquid? It's literally a mathematically guaranteed breakout.

Early replies (first 3)

62412459
>>62412453

I do use Polkadot everyday

Its on my autobuy

62412625
>>62412453
awaiting the next bridge exploit to completely drain the polkadot system yet again

62412713
>>62412625

This is impossible

Polkadot is one of the most secure cryptos

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62412947
>>62412713
look up what happened to acala and moonbeam, the biggest parachains

item.title: 1 BTC = 1 BTC — 4 replies — 16.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
1 BTC = 1 BTC

item.title: ITT we predict which businesses will go bankrupt over the next few years. — 13 replies — 12.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
ITT we predict which businesses will go bankrupt over the next few years.

Open thread • Replies: 13 • Updated: 2026-06-25 19:25 UTC

OP

ITT we predict which businesses will go bankrupt over the next few years. Companies that can't make the transition to the new system.

Early replies (first 3)

62409979
>>62409973
gamestop. bed bath and beyond. american multi-cinema. Pretty much anything else with dedicated shills on /biz/

62409980
>>62409973
Mstr is hnsing to 0 as we speak.

62409981
>>62409973
OpenGAYAY

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62409986
>>62409981
Gaysex too

62410043
I’m worried about telecom. I think they’re gonna get sued into oblivion and everyone will hop to Starlink (I know it sucks now, not glazing). It’s only a matter of time until normies realize fiber is the river that feeds all data centers and is the least secure part.

If people start blaming AI for their struggles, I expect manholes outside of data centers to be vandalized for years. It’s just a matter of time until people put two and two together. Deep bores and secure manholes are gonna be a very hot commodity.

Nobody is building these routes with security in mind. Just a crap load of redundancy. Redundancy is built on the idea that people won’t go around cutting fiber circuits, so it’s never going to be enough redundancy when this time comes.

If those telecom companies can’t hold up their end of the bargain, everyone is gonna jump ship. The long-haul transport fiber companies will be fine, but companies like Zayo are gonna struggle because their network was already bad and all the vandalism is gonna expose that.

The data centers I’ve worked in are only secure on the inside. The manholes that feed traffic in and out are all located around the perimeter. If I had $250M right now, I’d build a deep-bore secure ring connection that follows their mesh in a liberal city and just wait.

62410093
>>62409973
Not bankrupt but declining earnings failing dramatically in female beauty products due to more advanced filters and more women globally using technology to market themselves.
They’ll just stop using colored mud on their faces and evolve into more exhaustive liars.

62410519
>>62409973
SpaceX will go bankrupt when a rocket with people inside explodes on the launchpad. Screenshot this.

62410527
Too early on this one but daycare if ai actually gives back time to the workforce.

62410535
>>62410519
Tesla will likely go bankrupt when they are unable to adapt to a changing market, especially after elon goes to prison

62410537
just look at Tesla's quarterly profits

62410554
>>62409973
Openai, whirlpool, tesla, mstr, spacekeks

62412618
>>62409973
>>62409979
Five Guys, Hooters, Outback

62412645
i'm indian so this picture of a sub 5 foid gets me to reply because shes white

item.title: VOO and Chill — 75 replies — 25.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
VOO and Chill

Open thread • Replies: 75 • Updated: 2026-06-25 17:30 UTC

OP

The more I look into investing and compare the historical returns of certain ETFs / individual stocks it seems like the whole philosophy of “VOO and Chill” is equal parts really smart and retarded
It’s like a no brainer for money but even now as long as the market is in a growth period shouldn’t you at least do QQQ?
It seems so simple that’d you miss a lot of upside but the returns are honestly not bad and extremely consistent
How accurate is this assessment? It’s both so simple and guaranteed that everyone should it but also too safe so you end up missing out on more upside
I’m talking 5-20 years investing here not trading

Early replies (first 3)

62400515
Boomer funds are gay and lame

62400554
>>62400515
What qualifies as one
Why
What crypto do you want to shill

62400560
boglehead method is the tried and tested way, buy the market with an index fund that has a low fee, invest monthly, do it consistently for decades and you WILL be rich
but biz/anons don't care about being rich when they're older, they want to be rich NOW
greed is a deadly sin

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62405252
>>62405201
Are you replying to the wrong post all that guy did was ask questions

62405400
>>62404617
they predicted a 5-6% YTD gain for 2026 and we're already above that, lying shits.

62406764
>>62400493
Rather buy VT. Not so bullish on USA anymore

62406776
>>62406764
Still, conceptually it’s sound right?

62406804
>>62400493
>QQQ
if you want more returns (with more risk), you should do something like leverage + vt (or similar) instead of voo (you should at least do vti) and global small cap value, not sector etfs

62406811
>>62406804
in English doc

62406843
>>62406811
Alright! I'm gonna explain it like you're 5 years old!

Think of investing like choosing a basket of seeds: VOO is like only planting the biggest U.S. trees, VTI is like planting the whole U.S. garden, VT is like planting the whole world garden, and global small cap value is like adding lots of tiny, scrappy plants that have sometimes grown faster over long periods; if you want more possible growth and can stomach bigger ups and downs, using leverage is like turning up the volume on a diversified basket, which can help more than just buying one loud sector ETF, because sectors are a bet on one part of the economy and can stay out of favor for a long time, while broad diversification plus leverage is usually a cleaner way to aim for higher returns without relying on one slice of the market.

62406996
>>62406804
I am a noob and leverage seems scary desu like I will owe that money eventually right

62407040
>>62406996
yes, I'm not into leverage either, it evolves its own risks, but if you are not risk averse, it's better than gambling/prediction markts/crypto etc.

62407059
>>62400493
Voo used to pay more dividends before it was all fag7. Its basically the same as the nasdaq for the past decade

62407078
>>62400493
yeah I have like almost half a million in VTI right now, and I just need to sit on it for another 10 years. Its funny, I get excited whenever VTI goes down because it means i get a little bonus next deposit

62407086
Man I want to just have more principal to invest rather than chasing returns
Anyone know of good side hustles or AI grifts anyone can do
Idc as long as it’s triple digits a month at least

62407097
>>62406776
with Trump's economy + trade wars, VXUS actually went up a ton during the period and now a lot of countries are getting less and less dependent on America. If/when the AI bubble ever pops then you're going to be a lot more insulated since the American economy is being propped up by it.

62409216
>>62407097
Makes sense but aren’t all global economies just going to be downstream of American AI / Computing companies turning a profit?

62409221
>>62402005
pr34ch

62409463
>>62400515
>t. down 95% in fartniggercoin

62409479
>ETF and chill is smart but I'm smarter I can time the market!

62409545
>>62400493
honestly everything is fucked. index fund bagholders are forced to buy shitstocks and act like paypiggies for pump and dumpers like elon musk. it's literally causing a bubble. nobody wins except billionaires who force your grandma's retirement fund to pay for their malfunctioning AI.

62409811
>low fee index funds
it's VTI and VXUS for me

62409901
>>62400636
lol doomer, stop spreading negativity. you will be a lot happier even if you just invested 150 a month.

62410053
>>62409901
>Realism is negativity!

62410067
>>62400493
wtf are you doing bringing common sense into my retard and bot zoo?

62410086
>>62400636
>The problem is we have people starting at age 35 with like 150 a month thinking it's going to somehow mean something.
pop quiz! how much would you expect your money to grow over 150 months (12.5 years) with a conservative 6% annual return?

62410375
Yep just bought another share of VOO

62410405
>>62403053
They are also expecting SS and medicare to buff out their income and reduce their costs. Everyone here is likely planning to have no SS or other government pension.

62410414
>>62402995
>>62403044
Really you two are dumb as fuck. Do you not understand what FIRE is?

VOO has 15% CAGR since inception. 11.75% after inflation. With even a generous withdrawal rate you can live very comfortably on it if you have enough capital. My business partner does this, goes on vacations all the time. He has a couple of million invested across UK-equivalent ETFs like VUSA. Loads of people do this, it's not the absolute best use of capital but it's a good return for how liquid and low fee it is.
>>62403044
Learn to read you fucking imbecile. I posted that only millionaires CAN LIVE OFF IT. . Anyone can invest and make gains from ETFs but you won't get rich unless you invest for decades.
>Also getting rich quick is literally the point of all this
The point of what? Investing? No it isn't. The point of ETFs? LOL. You are just some dumb teen shitcoiner with sky high time preference I can just tell. Go and buy more shitcoins so you can "get rich quick" dickhead.

62410968
>>62410414
This is why I just wanna get as much capital in there as soon as possible it just seems like something I can actually control versus chasing returns
Don’t have any ideas though

62410981
>>62407086
how about a job nigger

62411192
>>62410981
I am currently at my full time salary job right now silly

62412068
VOO bros are up bigly today
